rag_rules: Assume statements must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Implementation-level signal routing bindings should be moved to the implementation annex.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of referenced elements.
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and use only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Validator diagnostics pointing to invalid assume/guarantee placements should be resolved by moving clauses to correct scopes or removing them.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the input AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations.
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments.
anti_patterns: Placing assume statements in component implementation scopes instead of component type scopes.
Using package-qualified identifiers or data types not defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics related to annex placement
Model fusion and scope preservation rules
